title: Executive Order 12708—Amendments to the Manual for Courts-Martial, United States, 1984

flags.justification: Narrow, technical procedural amendments to an existing military-justice code; no new broad authority is created.
Multiple distinct procedural amendments all coherently serve one system: court-martial procedure under the Uniform Code of Military Justice.
Exceptionally granular specificity throughout, with exact rule citations and exact procedural sequences.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
Standing-procedural-framework category applies: this cites three prior amending orders (12484, 12550, 12586), evidencing ongoing periodic revision.
Sec. 6 requires the Secretary of Defense to transmit a copy of this order to Congress under 10 U.S.C. §836, consistent with EO 12340's identical positive feature.
Extensive procedural rights are preserved and clarified throughout, including defense-counsel service requirements and waiver-relief provisions.
A routine, technical procedural amendment within an already-comprehensive system; no novel authority claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
